title: Britain’s New Leader Could Bring New Hope
text:
Britain’s New Leader Could Bring New Hope

Anatole Kaletsky

Gavekal Research | 22 Jun 2026

The removal of Sir Keir Starmer, Britain’s most unpopular prime minister since the start of modern polling, has been almost inevitable since early May (see Starmer’s Ruin Is Bullish For UK Assets), when the Labour Party suffered catastrophic losses in nationwide local elections. But Starmer’s expulsion from Downing Street became a near certainty last Friday, following the landslide victory of his rival Andy Burnham in a special election held on Thursday in a Manchester suburb. With reliable media reporters predicting that Starmer will announce his resignation this week, possibly as early as today, it makes sense for investors in UK assets to ask three immediate questions:Exactly a decade after the Brexit referendum justified unalloyed pessimism about Britain, it now seems appropriate to turn contrarian and offer some optimistic answers to the three questions above.
